full_prompt: Design a monolithic carbon-fiber-reinforced-aluminum-core (for this CAD prompt, treat as 7075-T73 aluminum equivalent isotropic material) pressurized pod shell section for a collegiate low-pressure transport pod benchmark on a host track (420 m length x 2.5 m inside diameter at 100 Pa absolute), operating at 100 km/h on the straight and 75 km/h on the curve per the benchmark Rules & Regulations v1.0. The pod shell is a single cylindrical body (no welded splices, no bonded longerons; all stiffeners integral) forming a 2.0 m long pressurized crew/payload section. External diameter 1.800 m (to clear the 2.500 m tube ID by 350 mm per side), internal diameter 1.790 m, wall thickness 5 mm including integral longitudinal + circumferential ring stiffeners (eight longitudinal tee-section stringers 25 mm wide x 40 mm tall x 3 mm wall spaced evenly; four circumferential ring frames at 400 mm spacing 30 mm wide x 35 mm tall x 3 mm wall). Integral end flanges: two ANSI B16.47 class B-style flanges, 1.900 m OD x 30 mm thick with a 24-bolt circle on 1.840 m PCD, hole diameter 18 mm for M16 bolts. Overall bounding envelope 1.900 m diameter x 2.120 m length. Material: 7075-T73 aluminum with Young's modulus 72 GPa, Poisson's ratio 0.33, density 2810 kg/m^3, yield 435 MPa, UTS 505 MPa, endurance limit 140 MPa at 5e8 cycles per MIL-HDBK-5J. Operating environment: external pressure 100 Pa absolute (near-vacuum), internal pressure 101325 Pa absolute (sea-level atmosphere) giving a delta-P of 101225 Pa internal pressurization; passenger+systems mass 400 kg distributed; cruise 100 km/h (27.8 m/s) with lateral 0.4 g at curve; thermal range -20 C to +60 C. Three load cases, all with the MANDATORY benchmark R&R safety factor of 2x on calculated load. Load Case 1 (internal pressurization): internal 101325 Pa abs, external 100 Pa abs gauge = delta-P +101225 Pa outward, with 2x factor applied -> design delta-P = 202450 Pa, both end flanges fully constrained axially. Load Case 2 (external crush fault): an accidental tube over-pressurization from 100 Pa to 200 kPa with the pod interior still at 101 kPa = delta-P -99 kPa inward, with 2x factor applied -> design inward delta-P = -198 kPa, both flanges fixed. Load Case 3 (curve cornering): 0.4g lateral inertia on 400 kg distributed mass plus 2g vertical bump at 75 km/h through a curve, applied as body-force on the shell + passenger mass, flanges fixed. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 factored internal pressurization, peak von Mises in the shell or stringer-frame intersection must not exceed 218 MPa (yield/2.0 again inside the already-factored load case is not double-dipped; apply yield/2.0 directly per the benchmark rulebook's 'SF 2x on calc load' phrasing so R1 limit is 218 MPa = 435/2.0); (R2) under LC2 factored external crush fault, peak stress 218 MPa AND a linear buckling eigenvalue analysis must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 2.0 (R2 explicitly tests the R&R mandatory SF2); (R3) under LC3 cornering, peak stress 218 MPa; (R4) first five natural frequencies in the empty shell must lie outside the dominant track-induced excitation band of 10-40 Hz (from joint spacing at 100 km/h, 3.5 m concrete panel joints); (R5) shell mass (the monolithic part) must not exceed 180 kg per the pod mass budget; (R6) fatigue: under 1000 pressure cycles (Evac + vent) the stress range at any stringer-frame intersection must remain below 140 MPa. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 geometry plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic pressurized pod shell section for a collegiate low-pressure transport pod (host track 420 m x 2.5 m ID at 100 Pa absolute, cruise 100 km/h straight / 75 km/h curve), treated as 7075-T73 aluminum equivalent isotropic. The shell is a single cylindrical body with integral longitudinal tee stringers and circumferential ring frames and two large bolted end flanges; no welded splices. The benchmark Rules and Regulations v1.0 mandate a 2x safety factor on the calculated load for every load case.

Envelope: ~1.9 m OD flanges, 2.12 m long, 1.8 m shell OD with a 5 mm wall including integral stiffeners.

Load cases.
- LC1 internal pressurization: delta-P +101225 Pa outward (interior 101325 Pa abs, exterior 100 Pa abs), both end flanges axially fixed; design load 2x.
- LC2 external crush fault: tube over-pressurized to 200 kPa with interior at ~101 kPa, delta-P -99 kPa inward; design load 2x, flanges fixed; also check linear buckling.
- LC3 curve cornering: 0.4 g lateral and 2 g vertical body force on shell plus 400 kg passenger/systems mass at 75 km/h, flanges fixed.
Also check first five natural frequencies against the 10-40 Hz track-joint excitation band and a 1000-cycle pressure-cycle fatigue margin at stringer-frame intersections.

prompt.title: Pressurized pod shell section for collegiate low-pressure transport pod benchmark
prompt.brief: Stiffened 7075-T73 cylindrical shell 1.8 m OD x 2.0 m long; tube at 100 Pa; pod at 101 kPa; benchmark mandatory SF 2x.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell (SHELL281) with solid 3D at flanges and frame-stringer intersections

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Pod-interior ECS outside this CAD prompt scope.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: External near-vacuum; aerodynamic drag handled by envelope drag model.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Composite layup for true CFRP shell

eval_coverage: benchmark mandatory SF 2x on calculated load
